steps.title: Match the present rule
steps.content: The sentence describes a current standing rule: Interpol can help with investigations, but only Algerian authorities have arrest power there. The blank needs a present-tense linking verb for authorities.

steps.title: Avoid past-tense shifts
steps.content: Choice A is past progressive, suggesting a temporary past situation. Choice B is simple past, and Choice C is past perfect; both wrongly move the rule into the past. The answer is D.
